gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/187)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/187)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ يَحْيَى الطَّحَّانِ عَمَّنْ حَدَّثَهُ

text: خَمْسٌ مِنْ فَوَاكِهِ الْجَنَّةِ فِي الدُّنْيَا الرُّمَّانُ الْإِمْلِيسِيُّ وَ التُّفَّاحُ الشَّيْسَقَانُ وَ السَّفَرْجَلُ وَ الْعِنَبُ الرَّازِقِيُّ وَ الرُّطَبُ الْمُشَانُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from his father, from Ahmad Bin Suleyman, from Ahmad Bin Yahya Al Tahhan, from the one who narrated it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abullahasws having said: ‘Five are from the fruits of the Paradise in the world – the Al-Amleysi Pomegranate, and Al-Sheysqan apple, and the Quince, and the Al-Raziqy grapes, and Al-Mushan dates’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from his father from Ahmad ibn Sulayman from Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Tahhan from those who narrated to him who has said the following: Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Five kinds of fruit are from paradise: Pomegranates of ‘Imlisiy, apples of al-Shaysaqan, quince, grapes of al-Raziqiy and fresh dates of al-Mushan.’”
